authors.name: Haddis Alemayehu
authors.bio: Haddis Alemayehu was an Ethiopian novelist. His Amharic novel Love to the Grave is considered a classic of modern Ethiopian literature. Aside from literature, he was the Minister of Foreign Affairs from 1960 to 1961.

books.title: Fəqər əskä Mäqabər (Love until Death)
books.description: It is one of the best known novels in Ethiopia and is considered a classic of Ethiopian literature featuring a tragic love story.

books.title: Tizzita (Memories)
books.description: When the Ethiopian government collapsed, he stood with Ras Imru, fighting for his freedom and for the love of his country that burned in his heart. Whether standing, sitting, or sleeping, this love melted his heart. His eyes filled with tears, and he wrote these words in his book "The Cry of Ethiopia," in the name of his mother country, to share his grief with his brothers and fellow Ethiopians.

books.title: Yelimzhat (Sweet Only in Dreams)
books.description: The main character of this "Yelm Zhat" book is a beautiful woman named Demechech Tzehay. Haddis shows us how this beautiful character "Demechech" was forced from the countryside and came to our capital, Addis Ababa, what kind of difficult life she lives in, and how she manages to earn a living by educating her only child (at the Teferi Mekonnen School).
